Standing at the top of the world, she felt a surge of emotions-pride, relief, and an overwhelming sense of achievement.

  1. Complete the sentence with a suitable reason.
    Bachendri Pal said that Arunima had conquered the Everest within her because ________.
  2. How might Arunima have felt after what Bachendri Pal told her?
  3. Choose a phrase from the extract which shows that reaching the summit of Mount Everest was tiring.
  4. Select the correct option to complete the sentence.
    After her success when Arunima felt ‘an overwhelming sense of achievement’, she would have most likely said, “________”
    1. I am confident of my abilities
    2. I will accomplish this soon
    3. I am excited to begin this climb
    4. I finally realised my dream
Solution
  1. She decided to climb Mount Everest with a prosthetic leg.
  2. Arunima might have felt confident and motivated after hearing Bachendri Pal’s words. She likely felt supported and encouraged, knowing that an experienced mountaineer believed in her abilities. This would have strengthened her determination to achieve her goal
  3. The phrase from the extract that shows that reaching the summit of Mount Everest was tiring is “after 52 exhausting days”.
  4. I finally realised my dream.
Q 2

What did Arunima do in order to fulfill her ambition of conquering Mount Everest?

Solution

To pursue her ambition, Arunima completed a basic mountaineering course at the Nehru Institute of Mountaineering in Uttarkashi. She then approached Bachendri Pal for guidance to help her fulfil her dream of climbing Mount Everest.

Q 3

Arunima is a beacon of hope and resilience. Support this statement with evidence from the text.

Solution

Arunima Sinha is considered a beacon of hope and resilience because she overcame a tragic accident that resulted in the loss of her leg and went on to achieve something extraordinary. Instead of giving up, she trained rigorously and became the first female amputee to climb Mount Everest. Her determination and courage inspire many people to face challenges with strength and never lose hope.

Q 4

Arunima’s autobiography is titled Born Again on the Mountain: A Story of Losing Everything and Finding It Back. Why do you think this title is appropriate?

Solution

The title of Arunima Sinha’s autobiography, “Born Again on the Mountain: A Story of Losing Everything and Finding it Back”, is appropriate because it reflects her journey of transformation and resilience.
Born Again: After losing her leg in a tragic accident, Arunima did not give up. Instead, she rebuilt her life with a new purpose, as if she was given a second chance.
On the Mountain: Mount Everest symbolises her triumph over adversity. Her journey to the peak represents her determination to overcome obstacles. Losing Everything and Finding it Back: She lost her leg and her sports career but found a new purpose through mountaineering, proving that determination can help reclaim one’s dream.

Q 5

Arunima says, “Failure is when we don’t have goals worthy enough.” What would be the most likely impact of this statement on readers?

Solution

Arunima’s statement inspires readers to set meaningful goals and view failure as a lack of ambition rather than defeat. It encourages determination, self-reflection, and a growth mindset. By’ emphasising the importance of worthy goals, it motivates readers to strive, for success despite challenges, making them more resilient and focused.

Q 1

In groups of four, select a role from 1-4 given below and speak about the views allotted to each. Use the prompts and vocabulary given. Take turns to express your opinions, agreement, and disagreement.
Role-Specific Prompts

  1. School Student: Talk about why you think Physical Education should be included in school activities.
  2. Parent: Discuss your concerns or support for your child participating in Physical Education activities.
  3. Physical Education Teacher: Share your experiences about Physical Education activities and why you think they are valuable.
  4. Sports Camp Organiser: Explain how you ensure safety and why you believe sports are beneficial for participants.

Prompts

  • In my opinion…
  • From my point of view…
  • One reason for this is…
  • I disagree with the notion that…
  • I support this perspective because…
  • I find it hard to agree because …
  • I believe that…
  • I disagree with…
  • I agree with the idea that..

Vocabulary you may use
precautions, unique, experiences, fun, safe, enjoyable, thrilling, builds, confidence, concern, explore, overcome fear, participate.

Solution
  1. School student: In my opinion
    Physical Education should be included in school activities because it builds confidence and helps students stay fit. One reason for this is that it makes learning fun and encourages teamwork.
  2. Parent: From my point of view, Physical Education is essential for children as it helps them explore their abilities and overcome fear. However, I have a concern about safety, so proper precautions should be taken.
  3. Physical Education Teacher: I believe that Physical Education is valuable because it provides unique experiences and makes students physically and mentally strong. I support this perspective because sports teach discipline and teamwork.
  4. Sports camp organiser: I agree with the idea that sports are beneficial as they are thrilling, safe and enjoyable. I ensure safety by taking proper precautions and training participants well before activities.

Q 1

  4. The everlasting flame suggests that the stories of courage and valour of our stories are also everlasting.

Q 2

I was happy to hear that you got an opportunity to visit the National War Memorial. I have heard a lot about it. Some people might see a war memorial as just a monument, statue, an edifice to celebrate a war or victory and commemorate those who sacrificed their lives or got injured in the war. The significance of the National War Memorial is that it tells us about the brave soldiers who made the supreme sacrifice while defending the country.

Lance Naik Albert Ekka was an example of selflessness because he charged the enemy bunker with complete disregard for his personal safety. It was his determination that he charged the enemy bunker, killed two soldiers and silenced the light Machine Gun. The students were awestruck to learn about the bravery of the war-heroes. They were also humbled to see that these great men gave their lives so that others in the country can live in peace.

sections.html:
Q 1

You have read about the sacrifices made by the Armed Forces during war. However, the Armed Forces also play a key role during peace time. Write a letter to your friend sharing how the Armed Forces contribute their services during the times of natural calamities (floods, earthquakes, etc.), infrastructure development (building bridges in remote areas, etc.) and for welfare initiatives (medical camps, establishment of libraries, etc.)

Solution

24, Civil Lines Kanpur
5 September 2025 Dear Rohan,
I hope this letter fin’ds you in the best of health and spirits. Today, I wanted to share something truly inspiring with you – the incredible contribution of our armed forces beyond the battlefield.
Did you know that during natural calamities like floods, earthquakes, or cyclones, the armed forces are often the first to reach the affected areas? They rescue people, provide food and shelter, and help restore normal life. Their courage and selflessness are truly admirable.
Apart from’ that, they also build bridges in remote and difficult terrains where even basic transport is a challenge. This helps villagers connect with nearby towns and access schools, hospitals, and markets. It’s amazing how they serve the country in so many ways!
The armed forces also organise medical camps in far-off areas where healthcare is not easily available. These camps provide free checkups, medicines, and even minor surgeries. It shows how dedicated they are to the welfare of every citizen.
I really feel proud and grateful for their service. They are true heroes in every sense. I thought of writing to you because I know you’d appreciate this as much as I do.
Take care and write back soon.

Your friend,
Vikram Bhatia.

Q 1

Every branch of the Armed Forces has a motto. Read the mottos given below.

Solution

Do it yourself.

Q 2

India has many other Forces that serve the nation. For example, Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF).
Find out about such Forces and discuss with the teacher.

Solution

Yes, India has several other forces apart from the Army, Navy, and Air Force that serve and protect the nation in various ways. Here are some of them:

  1. CRPF (Central Reserve Police Force) – Maintains law and order, handles internal security, and assists in disaster relief.
  2. BSF (Border Security Force) – Guards India’s international borders and prevents cross-border crimes.
  3. CISF (Central Industrial Security Force) – Provides security to important government installations, airports, metro systems, and public sector enterprises.
  4. ITBP (Indo-Tibetan Border Police) – Protects the border along the India-China border and operates in high-altitude areas.
  5. SSB (Sashastra Seema Bal) – Guards India’s borders with Nepal and Bhutan and prevents smuggling and trafficking.
  6. NSG (National Security Guard) – Also known as Black Cats, this elite force handles counter-terrorism and hostage rescue operations.
  7. Assam Rifles – One of the oldest paramilitary forces, mainly responsible for maintaining peace in the Northeast region.
  8. Coast Guard – Protects India’s maritime interests and conducts search and rescue operations at sea.

These forces play crucial roles in keeping India safe and functioning smoothly.

Q 3

Solution

Do it yourself.

Q 4

Solution

Many women have joined the armed forces and are proudly serving the nation with courage and dedication. They work in various roles such as pilots, engineers, doctors, and combat officers, proving their strength and skills alongside men. Their presence in the Army, Navy, and Air Force reflects progress towards gender equality and inspires young girls to dream big. These brave women not only protect the country but also break stereotypes, showing that patriotism and bravery know no gender.

The soldiers remain vigilant and dedicated, whether it’s windy, snowy, or 3. scorchin. hot. They walk on 4. lonely stretches of land like the yogis. Their duty takes them to different terrains, including mountains, valleys, 5. deserts, and marsh¬es. The poet acknowledges that they have given a major part of their 6. youth to protect the nation. The poet says the wind chimes show their respect to the soldiers’ 7. feat. The poet concludes with prayers for their well-being and blessings.

Q 2

The theme of the poem 'My Dear Soldiers'  is ________.

Q 3

The tone of the poem 'My Dear Soldiers' is ________.

  1. admiring
  2. sad
  3. respectful
  4. grateful
  5. prayerful
Q 4

Complete the following sentences by explaining why the poet has used, the following poetic devices.(Poem - My Dear Soldiers)

  1. Imagery because ________.
  2. Simile because the soldiers are likened to ________.
  3. Metaphor because the poet uses wind chimes ________.
  4. Repetition of the words ‘you’ and ‘we’ because ________.
  5. Alliteration such as ‘season or snowy’ and ________ to create ________.
Solution
  1. Imagery because the poet paints vivid pictures of the soldiers’ hardships and dedication through descriptive language.
  2. Simile because the soldiers are likened to yogis, emphasising their discipline and endurance.
  3. Metaphor because the poet uses wind chimes as a symbol of the nation’s gratitude and recognition of the soldiers’ achievements.
  4. Repetition of the words ‘you’ and ‘we’ because it emphasises the contrast between the soldiers’ sacrifices and the civilians’ peaceful lives.
  5. Alliteration such as ‘season or snowy’ and ‘scorching sun’ to create a rhythmic effect and emphasise the harsh conditions faced by the soldiers.

Q 1

Read the extract given below and answer the questions that follow.
When we are all asleep
You still hold on to your deed
Windy season or snowy days
Or scorching sun’s sweltering rays
You are there guarding all the time awake
Treading the lonely expanses as yogis

  1. Identify a set of opposite words from the extract.
  2. Complete the sentence appropriately. The phrase ‘still hold on to your deed’ refers to the soldier’s attitude of ________.
  3. Identify whether the following statement is true or false. The soldiers walk across the deserted stretches of land.
  4. Give evidence from the extract to show that soldiers face harsh weather conditions.
  5. Which quality of the soldiers is highlighted in this extract?
    1. modesty
    2. optimism
    3. simplicity
    4. dedication
Solution
  1. asleep and awake.
  2. The phrase ‘still hold on to your deed’ refers to the soldier’s attitude of unwavering duty and perseverance.
  3. True. (Evidence: “Treading the lonely expanses as yogis”)
  4. “Windy season or snowy days.
    Or scorching sun’s sweltering rays”. These lines give evidence that soldiers face harsh weather conditions.
  5. dedication
Q 2

Who is the speaker of this poem 'My Dear Soldiers'? How can you say so?

Solution

The speaker of the poem is A.P.J. Abdul Kalam. I can say so because his name is mentioned at the end of the poem.

Q 3

How is the theme of the poem 'My Dear Soldiers' conveyed through imagery? Support your answer with examples from the poem.

Solution

The theme is conveyed through vivid imagery, such as:

  • “Windy season or snowy days” (harsh weather).
  • “Treading the lonely expanses as yogis” (solitude and discipline).
  • “Climbing the heights or striding the valleys” (diverse terrains).
Q 4

How does the poet highlight the commitment of the soldiers? Provide examples from the poem to support your answer.

Solution

The poet highlights commitment through examples like:

  • “You still hold on to your deed” (perseverance).
  • “You are there guarding all the time awake” (constant vigilance).
  • “Prime of your youth given to the nation” (sacrifice of their best years).
Q 5

What is the significance of the line ‘Prime of your youth given to the nation!!’ with reference to sacrifice and service?

Solution

The line signifies the ultimate sacrifice of soldiers, when they are young, dedicating their strongest and most productive years to protect the nation.

Q 6

The poem ends with a prayer for the soldiers’ well-being and blessings. Why is it important for each one of us to pray for the well-being of soldiers?

Solution

Praying for soldiers shows gratitude, moral support, and recognition of their sacrifices, ensuring they feel valued and motivated.

Q 2

Complete the table given below based on your understanding of the story. An example has been done for you. Share your answers with your classmates and the teacher.

Cause Effect
1. Ullal had been captured by the Portuguese. (i) Ullal had to pay a tribute to the colonial power.
2. (ii) Rani Abbakka's husband Veera Narsimha felt it was a hasty decision.
3. Veera Narsimha ordered the soldiers to stop Rani Abbakka when she said she would not be frightened by the Portuguese. (iii)
4. Rani Abbakka addressed a meeting of rajas for their support, who felt they would be no match for the Portuguese army and their power. (iv)
5. (v) Rani Abbakka in turn attacked the Portuguese Fort.
Solution
Cause Effect
1. Ullal had been captured by the Portuguese. (i) Ullal had to pay a tribute to the colonial power.
2. Rani Abbakka refused to pay tribute to the Portuguese and stopped selling them spices and cloth. (ii) Rani Abbakka's husband Veera Narsimha felt it was a hasty decision.
3. Veera Narsimha ordered the soldiers to stop Rani Abbakka when she said she would not be frightened by the Portuguese. (iii) The soldiers, inspired by Abbakka's bravery, refused to obey the order.
4. Rani Abbakka addressed a meeting of rajas for their support, who felt they would be no match for the Portuguese army and their power. (iv) Their lack of support strengthened Abbakka's resolve to fight the .Portuguese alone.
5. The Portuguese attacked Ullal in response to Rani Abbakka's defiance. (v) Rani Abbakka in turn attacked the Portuguese Fort.

  1. In this context, ‘tribute’ refers to a forces, payment that the Portuguese demanded from Ullal as a sign of submission to their colonial power.

Rani Abbakka’s words to her mother on her deathbed would have made her mother feel proud, comforted and hopeful, knowing her daughter would protect their kingdom bravely.

The people of Ullal and the soldiers were brave, loyal and proud of their kingdom, ready to defend Ullal’s freedom at any cost. Their support showed their trust in Rani Abbakka and their strong desire to resist Portuguese rule.

The people of Ullal must have felt overjoyed and proud when the mighty Portuguese were defeated. They would have celebrated their freedom and admired Rani Abbakka’s bravery.

Rani Abbakka’s victory showed that even a powerful foreign force could be defeated with courage and unity. This would have inspired other rulers to stand up against foreign powers and fight for their freedom.

Q 1

India’s past was heroic, and at present, the pace of her development is inspiring. Women play a significant role in it. These women are around us, inspiring tts all the time.
With these thoughts, write a conversation between two students, Pratap and Tarana, discussing the role played by women in forming India’s identity as a strong nation.
You may begin like this:
Pratap: Tarana, I have a lot of respect for women like…
Tarana: I believe in the capabilities of women. Did you …?
Complete the conversation in about six to eight sentences for each of them and share your answers with your classmates and the teacher.

Solution

Pratap: Tarana, I have a lot of respect for women like Rani Lakshmibai, Sarojini Naidu, and Kalpana Chawla. They have made significant contributions in different fields.
Tarana: I believe in the capabilities of women. Did you know that women were actively involved in India’s freedom struggle and continue to shape our nation today?
Pratap: Yes! Apart from freedom fighters, women like Indira Gandhi and Kiran Bedi have played vital roles in politics and governance.
Tarana: Absolutely! Today, women are excelling in science, sports, business, and even space exploration. Look at Mary Kom, who has made India proud in boxing.
Pratap: And don’t forget women like Dr. Tessy Thomas, the ‘Missile Woman of India,’ who has contributed to India’s defence technology.
Tarana: Yes! Women are proving that they are equal to men in every field. Their courage and determination are helping India grow stronger every day.
Pratap: We must support and encourage them so that they continue to break barriers and inspire future generations.
Tarana: Exactly! A nation can only be truly strong when men and women work together with equality and respect.

sections.label: Let us explore
sections.html:
Q 1

In groups of five, find facts about any one brave woman who we remember because of her achievements and present in class. For example, Rani Chennamma of Kittur, Rani Durgavati, Ahilyabai Holkar, Jhalkari Bai, Captain Lakshmi Sahgal, Kanaklata Barua, Kalpana Chawla, or anyone else.
Include the following in your presentation:

  1. Childhood
  2. Training / Education
  3. Achievements
  4. Contributions
  5. What you have learnt from her
Solution

Brave Woman: Kalpana Chawla

  1. Childhood: Kalpana Chawla was born on March 17, 1962, in Karnal, Haryana. From a you,ng age, she was fascinated by aeroplanes and space. She would often look up at the sky and dream of flying.
  2. Training /Education: Kalpana stud¬ied engineering and earned a degree in aero¬nautical engineering from Punjab Engineer¬ing College. She later moved to the United States to continue her education and earned a master’s and Ph.D. in aerospace engineering.
  3. Achievements: She became the first Indian-origin woman to travel to space. In 1997, she flew on her first space mission aboard the Space Shuttle Columbia. She went to space again in 2003, but unfortunately, the shuttle broke apart while returning to Earth, and she lost her life along with her crew.jnembers.
  4. Contributions: Kalpana Chawla proved that with hard work and determination, anyone can achieve their dreams. She inspired many young girls to pursue careers in science, technology, and space exploration.
  5. What I have learnt from her: Kalpana Chawla’s life teaches us to dream big, work hard, and never give up. She showed us that no goal is too high if we are willing to put in the effort and dedication. Her courage and passion for space continue to inspire people all over the world.
